creator: indiamachinemart.in@gmail.com
text:
The issues you're encountering while building devel/py-libzfs on FreeBSD 14.1-RELEASE are primarily due to macro redefinitions and missing type definitions. To resolve the macro redefinition warnings, you can modify the code to check if the likely and unlikely macros are already defined before defining them again. This can be done by wrapping the macro definitions in #ifndef guards as follows:


#ifndef likely
#define likely(x) __builtin_expect((x), 1)
#endif

#ifndef unlikely
#define unlikely(x) __builtin_expect((x), 0)
#endif
Inserting these guards in your code will prevent the redefinition warnings. For the errors related to pool_raidz_expand_stat_t and ZPOOL_CONFIG_RAIDZ_EXPAND_STATS, ensure that these types and constants are defined and included properly in your build environment. If they are part of the OpenZFS code, make sure to include the appropriate headers. For example:


#include <sys/zfs_context.h>
#include <sys/fs/zfs.h>
If the headers are correctly included but the types and constants are still undefined, it may indicate changes or omissions in the OpenZFS code on FreeBSD 14.1. In such a case, reviewing the OpenZFS documentation or source code to identify the correct definitions and ensure they are included in your build environment might be necessary. These steps should help resolve the compilation issues and allow you to successfully build py-libzfs.

Regards 
Deepanshu Garg
